issue.title: `is_consteval_only` preconditions are too weak

discussion.p: The precondition for `is_consteval_only` is insufficient. A definitive false answer requires every type that `T` is compounded from to be complete or cv void.

li.p: Combine p4-5 into one paragraph with a bullet list, then add a new bullet, as indicated:

blockquote.p: -4- For the purpose of defining the templates in this subclause,:

li.p: (4.1) — a function call expression declval<T>() for any type `T` is considered to be a trivial (, ) function call that is not an odr-use () of `declval` in the context of the corresponding definition notwithstanding the restrictions of .
(4.2) — -5- For the purpose of defining the templates in this subclause, let VAL<T> for some type `T` be an expression defined as follows:

p.ins: (4.3) — let `C(T)` be the set of types defined as follows:

(4.3.3) — otherwise, if `T` is the type "`noexcept`opt function of parameter-type-list cv-qualifier-seqopt ref-qualifieropt returning `U`", then `C(T)` is the union of `C(U)` and `C(X)` for each type `X` in the parameter-type-list;

(4.3.4) — otherwise, if remove_cv_t<T> is a complete class type, then `C(T)` is the union of the sets `C(U)`, for each `U` that is the type of a (possibly indirect) non-static data member of remove_cv_t<T>;
(4.3.5) — otherwise, `C(T)` is the set containing the single element remove_cv_t<T>.

li.p: Modify , Table 54 — Type property predicates as indicated:

td.del: remove_all_extents_t<T> shall be a complete type or cv `void`.
td.ins: If `C(T)` does not contain `std::meta::info`, then it shall not contain an incomplete class type.

issue.resolution: This issue is resolved by the resolution of issue .
